title: Curtea Supremă i-a anihilat lui Trump arma taxelor vamale
summary: Francis Fukuyama analyzes the US Supreme Court's decision to strike down Trump's tariffs imposed under IEEPA, arguing it fatally undermined his ability to use tariffs as arbitrary political weapons. He highlights how Trump used discretionary tariff power for personal enrichment and political coercion, and argues the ruling is a victory for institutional governance both domestically and internationally.
talking_points: The Supreme Court's annulment of IEEPA-based tariffs removed Trump's ability to set arbitrary, country-specific tariff rates as political leverage
Trump treated tariffs not as economic instruments but as personal political weapons, exemplified by the Switzerland episode where tariffs were reduced after receiving luxury gifts
Trump's presidency left virtually no institutional legacy, relying on executive orders and ad-hoc deals rather than Congressional legislation
The fallback authority under Section 122 limits tariffs to a uniform rate and expires after 150 days, drastically reducing presidential discretion
Arbitrary tariff-setting power invited corruption, with foreign partners offering gifts and concessions to secure favorable trade terms
author: Francis Fukuyama
